problem.instruction: Fill in each of the blanks ① and ② with the ONE most appropriate word.
problem.passage:
English is known to have only eight inflectional suffixes as listed in (1), which implies that other suffixes are all derivational.

(1) Inflectional suffixes in English
a. Noun: Plural -s / Possessive -'s
b. Verb: Past tense -ed / Past participle -en/-ed / Progressive -ing / 3rd person singular present -s
c. Adjective: Comparative -er / Superlative -est

First, inflectional suffixes do not change the grammatical [BLANK_①] of the stem, while some derivational suffixes can change it as heart, a noun, becomes an adjective after -less attaches. Secondly, a derivational suffix must combine with the root or the base before an inflectional suffix. This is why neighbor-hood-s is attested but neighbor-s-hood is not. Lastly, the semantic contribution of an inflectional suffix is usually transparent and consistent. These examples suggest that the [BLANK_②] of an inflectional suffix is predictable, while that of a derivational suffix may not be predictable.

blanks.label: ①
blanks.context: do not change the grammatical [①] of the stem

blanks.label: ②
blanks.context: the [②] of an inflectional suffix is predictable

steps.label: 범위 설정 — 발문 조건 확인
steps.explanation: 형태론(morphology) 영역. inflectional suffix vs derivational suffix의 세 가지 구별 기준을 다루는 지문. 빈칸 ①②는 각각 서로 다른 기준에 해당하며 ONE word 조건 적용. ①은 '품사/문법적 속성' 기준, ②는 '의미 예측성' 기준임을 앞뒤 문맥으로 파악.

steps.label: 이론 ↔ 지문 대응 — 3기준 매핑
steps.explanation:
Fromkin 3기준 ↔ 지문 단락 대응:
기준1(품사): '굴절 접사 → 품사 불변' ↔ 'do not change the grammatical [①] of the stem'
기준2(순서): '파생→굴절 순' ↔ 'neighbor-hood-s ✓ / neighbor-s-hood ✗'
기준3(의미): '굴절 의미 예측 가능' ↔ 'the [②] of an inflectional suffix is predictable'

steps.label: 후보 소거 — 정확한 용어 특정
steps.explanation:
① 후보: class / form / function / category
→ 'grammatical ___ of the stem' 구조. 표준 언어학 용어는 'grammatical category' (= syntactic category = 품사). Fromkin Ch.4 표준 표현. ① = category.

② 후보: semantics / sense / meaning
→ 앞 문장 'semantic contribution'을 ONE word로 환언 = meaning. 'semantics'는 학문명이므로 부적절. ② = meaning.

steps.label: 확정 근거 — 지문 직접 근거
steps.explanation:
① 확정: 'inflectional suffixes do not change the grammatical ___ of the stem' + 대비 'heart(N) → adjective after -less' = 품사 변화 = category 변화. ① = category.
② 확정: 'semantic contribution of an inflectional suffix is usually transparent and consistent. These examples suggest that the ___ of an inflectional suffix is predictable' → 'semantic contribution'의 단어 환언 = meaning. ② = meaning.

steps.label: 전략 메모
steps.explanation: 형태론 굴절/파생 구별 출제 패턴: 3기준(품사·순서·의미) 중 빈칸이 어느 기준에 해당하는지 단락 위치로 먼저 확정. 앞 단락의 핵심 명사('semantic contribution')가 빈칸의 정답 후보가 되는 경우 많음. ONE word 조건이면 합성 표현(예: 'syntactic category') 불가 → 단어 하나(category, meaning)로 범위 좁힘.

key_terms.definition_en: A bound morpheme that marks grammatical categories (tense, number, case, degree) without changing the syntactic category or core meaning of the base.
key_terms.definition_ko: 굴절 형태소 — 시제·수·격 등 문법 범주 표시. 어기의 품사·핵심 의미 불변.
key_terms.exam_usage: 지문 전체 주제: 굴절 접사의 3가지 특성 설명

key_terms.definition_en: The part-of-speech class assigned to a word (noun, verb, adjective, adverb, etc.); also called syntactic category.
key_terms.definition_ko: 문법 범주 (= 통사 범주) — 품사 분류: 명사, 동사, 형용사 등.
key_terms.exam_usage: ① 빈칸: 굴절 접사가 바꾸지 않는 것 = grammatical category

key_terms.definition_en: Derivational morphemes must attach to the base before inflectional morphemes: [[base + deriv] + inflect]. Attested: nation-al-iz-ation-s; Unattested: *nation-s-al.
key_terms.definition_ko: 접사 순서 제약 — 파생 접사는 굴절 접사 안쪽(어기에 더 가까이) 위치. neighbor-hood-s ✓ / *neighbor-s-hood ✗.

references.author: Fromkin, V., Rodman, R., & Hyams, N.
references.title: An Introduction to Language (11th ed.)
references.excerpt: Inflectional morphemes do not change the syntactic category or the basic meaning of the word… Derivational morphemes may change the syntactic category and/or the meaning of the base.

references.author: Dept. of Linguistics, Ohio State Univ.
references.title: Language Files (12th ed.)
references.excerpt: The meaning contribution of inflectional affixes is predictable and consistent across all words to which they attach.

source.concept_explanation:
Three criteria distinguishing inflectional from derivational morphemes (Fromkin 2018):

① Category: Inflectional morphemes do not change the syntactic category of the stem.
  (품사 불변: 굴절은 어기의 품사를 바꾸지 않음. 파생은 가능 — teach [V] → teacher [N].)

② Ordering: Derivational morphemes must attach before inflectional morphemes.
  Attested: nation-al-iz-ation-s ✓   Unattested: *neighbor-s-hood ✗
  (순서 제약: 파생 접사는 굴절 접사 안쪽에 위치.)

③ Meaning: The meaning contribution of inflectional morphemes is transparent and predictable; derivational meaning may be idiosyncratic.
  (의미 예측성: 굴절 의미는 예측 가능. 파생은 어휘 특이적 — actor ≠ professor.)

Ordering constraint: the rule that derivational morphemes must attach to a stem before inflectional morphemes. Grammatical category (syntactic category): the syntactic class (N, V, Adj, etc.) of a word or stem.

questions.subject: linguistics
questions.connection_note: ordering 기준 집중. neighbor-hood-s 예시 공유.

questions.subject: linguistics
questions.connection_note: root · base · stem 정의. 선행 지식.
